extraction.one_line_summary: VINGEGAARD attacked the TT on a road bike, gaining 1:38 on POGAČAR who rode a TT bike throughout, extending his GC lead to 1:48.
tactical_patterns: VINGEGAARD rode road bike entire course including final climb, sacrificing early speed for climbing watts, reversing typical TT bike strategy.
POGAČAR used TT bike all way to finish on 9.4% gradient, suggesting team miscalculated aerodynamic vs weight penalty tradeoff point.
YATES Adam quietly moved into podium position while RODRÍGUEZ Carlos and HINDLEY Jai lost time, exposing GC depth fragility mid-tour.
Multiple early DSM crashes at first corner suggest recon failure; WELSFORD, team rider both fell same turn immediately after start.

rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: positive: gained 52 seconds at checkpoint three alone, demonstrating superior power sustainability on road bike setup versus pure aero gains
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: negative: second-fastest by huge margin but lost 1:38, indicating TT bike choice on steep finish was tactical error despite dominant power
rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: positive: best sprinter time at 36:07, holding form despite mountain stages suggesting unusual diesel engine capacity for classics rider
rider_signals.CAVAGNA Rémi: neutral: competitive early splits but faded on climb, confirming pure TT specialists lose advantage when gradients exceed 8 percent
rider_signals.YATES Adam: positive: jumped to third overall while others hemorrhaged time, revealing hidden TT strength or superior equipment dialing for hybrid course
rider_signals.RODRÍGUEZ Carlos: negative: caught by POGAČAR despite two-minute head start, losing GC position suggesting young rider cracking under stage race accumulation
rider_signals.WELSFORD Sam: negative: crashed immediately after late start gate departure, compounding poor positioning with bike handling failure under pressure
